steps.title: Plot the scatterplot points
steps.content: The points form a roughly straight increasing pattern. A fast Desmos check is to enter representative points from the scatterplot, then overlay the answer choices.

steps.title: Compare the linear choices first
steps.content: $y=-582+1.2x$ has positive slope and runs through the middle of the data. For example, at $x=3500$, it predicts $-582+1.2(3500)=3618$, close to the plotted value near $3700$. The equation $y=582-1.2x$ has negative slope, so it trends the wrong direction.

steps.title: Reject the exponential choices
steps.content: The exponential models do not match the scatterplot because the data do not curve upward like exponential growth; they stay close to a straight line. The negative exponential model is also negative for positive $x$, which is nowhere near the positive data values. These exponential choices are better eliminated by shape instead of graphing them on the thousand-scale $x$-axis.

steps.title: Confirm the model
steps.content: The only model with the correct linear shape, positive slope, and reasonable predictions is $y=-582+1.2x$. A is correct.
